decisions.briefing: Markets ended essentially flat today — NASDAQ -0.2% and S&P 500 -0.1% — a low-conviction session with no directional edge. Cash sits at $477.14, just below the $500 deployment threshold, so no new buys are triggered by the rules even though 4 slots remain open. Existing positions are held; top-scoring momentum candidates DKNG, WDAY, TTAN, and MSFT are flagged for entry if cash or a position exit creates room tomorrow.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities drifted slightly lower on Friday as investors remained cautious ahead of key economic data releases next week. Technology led modest declines, with the NASDAQ slipping 0.2% as rate-sensitive growth names paused after recent strong runs. Broader market breadth was mixed, reflecting a consolidation phase rather than any decisive trend break — typical ahead of quarter-end rebalancing flows.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市週五小幅收低，投資者在下週關鍵經濟數據公布前保持謹慎態度。科技股領跌，那斯達克指數下跌0.2%，近期漲勢強勁的成長型股票出現停頓。整體市場漲跌互見，反映出一種整固態勢，而非明確的趨勢突破——這在季末資金再平衡流動前十分常見。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance cost of holding uninvested cash in a portfolio. When your cash earns near-zero returns while the market rises, that idle money 'drags' down your overall return. Today illustrates a nuanced flip side: with only $477 in cash and a flat market, deploying into new positions at poor entry points can be worse than sitting tight — cash drag is real, but forced buying is riskier.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合中持有未投資現金所帶來的表現損耗。當您的現金幾乎零回報，而市場持續上漲時，這些閒置資金就會「拖累」整體報酬。今天的情況說明了另一面：手持477美元現金且市場走勢平淡，在不理想的時機強行進場，往往比靜待機會更危險——現金拖累固然存在，但被動式買入的風險更高。
